title: Tetradium sambucinum
intro: Tetradium sambucinum, also known as Evodia or Wu Zhu Yu, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and supporting cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Tetradium sambucinum include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, as well as being incorporated into herbal formulations such as capsules and teas.
taxonomy: Tetradium sambucinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant is native to East Asia and is also known by its alternative name, Evodia sambucina. The genus Tetradium is part of the Rutaceae family and is primarily found in Asia. The plant is known for its small white flowers and is often used in traditional medicine in its native region. Tetradium sambucinum is also used as a food ingredient in some East Asian cuisines.
uses: Tetradium sambucinum, also known as Chinese evodia, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of nausea and vomiting, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Additionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. However, its effectiveness and safety for human use require further scientific research and validation.

constituents: Tetradium sambucinum is a plant species that contains a variety of active constituents, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The main active constituents of Tetradium sambucinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have potential therapeutic effects on various health conditions. The combination of these active constituents in Tetradium sambucinum contributes to its traditional use in folk medicine for treating various health conditions.
